summary: Bootstrapped an initial decomp-toolkit/Ninja build skeleton for the GALE01 version. The PR imported dtk-template-style tooling, added GALE01 dtk config, section splits, build SHA checking, and a large generated symbols table, with the stated goal of enabling future merges from dtk-template and future PRs that add splits until dtk catches up to the existing make toolchain. At merge time the new Ninja path produced only empty 0.00% progress, so this was infrastructure groundwork rather than a completed migration.
what_changed: Added a root-level configure.py that accepts configure/progress modes, supports --version, --build-dir, --compilers, --map, --debug, --build-dtk, --sjiswrap, --verbose, and non-Windows --wrapper, then calls tools.project.generate_build or calculate_progress.
Established GALE01 as the only configured game version and wired config paths to config/GALE01/config.yml and config/GALE01/build.sha1.
Pinned tool/compiler inputs used by the skeleton: compilers_tag 20231018, dtk_tag v0.6.2, sjiswrap_tag v1.1.1, and wibo_tag 0.6.9.

Added base Metrowerks/GameCube compiler and linker flag configuration in configure.py, including GC/1.2.5n linker version, -proc gekko, -align powerpc, -O4,p, -Cpp_exceptions off, -RTTI off, -fp hardware, -str reuse, and include paths for include and build/GALE01/include.

Added tools/project.py, a project-agnostic dtk-template generator that writes build.ninja and objdiff.json, downloads or builds required tools, emits Ninja rules for MWCC, MWCC with sjiswrap, MWLD linking, elf2dol, REL generation, host builds, SHA checking, and progress.json generation.
Added tools/download_tool.py to fetch dtk, sjiswrap, wibo, and compiler archives from release URLs or files.decomp.dev, handling platform/architecture suffixes and executable permissions.
Added tools/transform_dep.py to convert Wine/Windows-style MWCC .d dependency paths into normal Unix/WSL paths so MWCC -MMD dependencies can be used outside native Windows.
Added tools/upload_progress.py to publish generated progress.json data to a frogress-compatible endpoint with git timestamp and commit hash metadata.
Added Google's ninja_syntax.py helper for Python build.ninja generation.
Added config/GALE01/config.yml pointing dtk at orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol, the known SHA1 hash, symbols.txt, splits.txt, and mw_comment_version 8.
Added config/GALE01/splits.txt defining initial DOL sections with align:8 and types for .init, extab, extabindex, .text, .ctors, .dtors, .rodata, .data, .bss, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2.
Added config/GALE01/symbols.txt with 33139 dtk symbol records covering functions, labels, and objects, including known startup/runtime names and many placeholder address-based names.
Added config/GALE01/build.sha1 with the expected SHA1 for build/GALE01/main.dol.
Updated .gitignore for dtk/Ninja artifacts such as .ninja_*, build.ninja, objdiff.json, orig/*/* with .gitkeep exceptions, and root-level /*.txt.
Updated .gitattributes and .editorconfig to normalize dtk-generated config text files to LF and tab indentation under config/GALE01/*.txt.
smart_moves: Kept imported dtk-template scripts recognizable and project-agnostic, with comments requesting changes be upstreamed to encounter/dtk-template; this reduces future divergence and supports the PR body's explicit goal of enabling future template merges.

Separated project-specific configuration into configure.py while keeping the shared generator in tools/project.py, making future decomp PRs more likely to adjust version/object/compiler settings without editing shared build logic.
Started with an empty config.libs list and an explicit note that Ninja reports 0.00% progress, making the skeleton safe to land as infrastructure without claiming matching progress prematurely.
Pinned external tool versions so reproducibility does not depend on latest GitHub releases.
Added SHA checking early via config/GALE01/build.sha1, so future dtk/Ninja output can be validated against the known main.dol hash.
Handled cross-platform compiler execution from the start: wibo auto-download on Linux, wine fallback for non-Windows, sjiswrap support, and dependency-file path transformation.
Normalized dtk-generated config text line endings and indentation to avoid noisy churn in large generated files like symbols.txt and splits.txt.
Included objdiff.json and progress.json generation as part of the skeleton, preparing the project for matching-status tracking even before source splits were migrated.
decomp_lessons: A dtk migration can be staged as a no-progress skeleton first: land config.yml, splits.txt, symbols.txt, build.ninja generation, SHA checking, and progress plumbing before moving real object splits over.

Keep dtk-template-derived code isolated from project-specific configuration; future projects or PRs can reuse tools/project.py-style shared code while only editing configure.py and config/<version>/ files.
For generated symbol inventories, use explicit type, size, scope, and data annotations in comments so the file remains useful for splitting and objdiff even when many symbols are still unnamed.
Line-ending policy matters for tooling-generated files. Explicit .gitattributes entries such as config/**/*.txt text eol=lf prevent platform-specific churn.
If MWCC is invoked through Wine/wibo, dependency files may contain Windows paths; a path transformation step is needed for Ninja deps to work reliably on Unix-like hosts.
Progress reporting can be wired before progress exists. The PR's sample output demonstrates a valid zero-progress baseline: 0 / 73 files, 0 / 3882080 code bytes, and 0 / 1211707 data bytes.
Future PRs adding dtk splits should update config.libs/Object lists and dtk split configuration incrementally, then use configure.py progress mode and SHA checking to verify behavior.
naming_conventions: Game-version configuration lives under config/GALE01/ with config.yml, splits.txt, symbols.txt, and build.sha1.
Unknown functions in symbols.txt use address-based names of the form fn_80003244 or fn_80005A30.

Known runtime/startup symbols keep original-style names when identified, such as memset, memcpy, __fill_mem, __start, __init_registers, __init_data, __init_hardware, __flush_cache, _rom_copy_info, and _bss_init_info.
Exception table symbols use hidden local @etb_<address> names for extab and @eti_<address> names for extabindex.
dtk symbol records use section:address assignment plus comment metadata such as type:function, type:object, type:label, size:0x..., scope:global/local/weak, hidden, and data:4byte.
configure.py defines Matching = True and NonMatching = False aliases for future object status declarations, although no objects were configured in this PR.
Helper constructors in configure.py use DolphinLib(lib_name, objects) for normal Dolphin libraries and Rel(lib_name, objects) for REL script objects.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added dtk split configuration for all initial DOL sections with align:8 and section types, establishing the section map needed before matching object-level splits.
Used build.sha1 as an output correctness check for build/GALE01/main.dol rather than relying only on progress percentages.
Configured Metrowerks flags for the intended matching environment, including GC/1.2.5n linker, -proc gekko, -O4,p, -inline auto, -fp hardware, -str reuse, -multibyte, and NDEBUG/DEBUG switching.
Provided MWCC and MWCC sjiswrap Ninja rules so future source files can opt into Shift-JIS wrapping when needed.
Generated objdiff.json from the build configuration, preparing future object matching to integrate with objdiff once real splits and objects are added.
The PR body explicitly states future PRs should add splits to the config until dtk catches up to the make toolchain.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No reviewer-requested naming or matching changes are evidenced in this dump.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add config.libs entries or Object lists to configure.py after the dtk skeleton.
Find PRs that update config/GALE01/splits.txt from the initial section-only skeleton to real object splits.
Find PRs that modify tools/project.py and check whether changes were upstreamed to encounter/dtk-template.
Find PRs that advance dtk_tag beyond v0.6.2 or adjust compilers_tag/wibo_tag/sjiswrap_tag.
Find PRs that use Matching and NonMatching object declarations in the Ninja/dtk build path.
Find PRs where build.sha1 or dtk SHA checking caught a mismatch.
